label: remove central restoring spring — kills sync collapse
notes: Diagnosis of the apparent 'collapse': with damping=0 motion is energetically conserved, but central restoring_k=0.45 was 9× stronger than gravity G=0.05, so each body acted as a 3D harmonic oscillator with the same period (T = 2π/√(0.45) ≈ 9.4s). Equal periods → bodies sync in phase → all three pass through origin together periodically, which reads as collapse. Fix: removed the central spring (restoring_k = 0). Only mutual gravity + escape_r pull-back wall remain. No preferred center → no synchronized origin-return. True 3-body dynamics. Centroid now drifts inside the outer wall instead of being pinned at origin. If clustering still emerges from gravitational binary formation, layer system-spread maintenance on top later.
